article_meta.title: SCOTUS Pro-Life Center Takes Its Fight Against Democrat Lawfare To The Supreme Court By: Shawn Fleetwood November 21, 20

reasoning: The model has several issues: (1) Attribution rule violations - 'bully us', 'overtly hostile to the mission of pregnancy centers', and 'harass and persecute First Choice for its protected speech' are all from quoted sources (Aimee Huber and Lincoln Wilson), not the author's prose. These are clear attribution rule violations. (2) The model correctly identifies 'seemingly political inquiry' as author prose bias - this is a genuine instance. (3) 'Democrat lawfare' in the headline is the author's own framing and is a legitimate bias flag, though the label 'Political Labeling' is not in the approved list. (4) The model uses non-standard bias type labels ('Political Labeling', 'Loaded Language', 'Framing Bias') instead of the approved types. (5) The model misses several clear instances of author-level bias: 'pro-life' used consistently as descriptor (Word Choice), 'fight back against' (Word Choice/Spin), 'New Jersey Democrats' lawfare' in author prose (Word Choice), 'gearing up to fight back' (Spin), the overall Slant of the article giving almost exclusively the pro-life center's perspective.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Three of five flagged items ('bully us', 'overtly hostile to the mission of pregnancy centers', 'harass and persecute First Choice for its protected speech') are from quoted or clearly attributed source statements. This is a systematic pattern of attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Of 5 flagged items, 3 are attribution violations (flagging source language as bias). 'Democrat lawfare' and 'seemingly political inquiry' are legitimate flags of author prose. So 3 out of 5 are false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches 'seemingly political inquiry' and 'Democrat lawfare' but misses several clear author-level bias patterns: 'gearing up to fight back against' (Spin), the overall one-sided Slant of the article, 'lawfare' repeated in author prose (Word Choice), and the sympathetic framing throughout including the client testimonial section without counterbalancing perspectives.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model uses labels not in the approved list: 'Political Labeling', 'Loaded Language', and 'Framing Bias' are not among the 15 valid bias types. These should have been mapped to Word Choice, Spin, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, or Slant.
